name: Dilmun
description: Dilmun, often described as a paradise, holds a prominent place in Sumerian mythology. It is depicted as a pure, bright, and disease-free land, a stark contrast to the toils and mortality of the mortal realm. The land was considered a divine abode, a place where gods dwelled and where the first humans were created. It was thought to be located east of Sumer, possibly an island or a coastal region in the Persian Gulf. Dilmun was associated with immortality and abundance, a place where death and sickness were unknown. It played a crucial role in various myths, notably the story of Enki and Ninhursag, where the creation of various deities and the eventual introduction of decay is described. The quest for immortality often led heroes to seek out Dilmun, solidifying its status as a mythical land of perfection and a symbol of humanity's longing for eternal life and a world without suffering. The location of Dilmun has been a subject of debate among scholars, with some identifying it with modern-day Bahrain or the eastern coast of Saudi Arabia. Regardless of its precise geographical location, its enduring presence in Sumerian literature and religious beliefs demonstrates its profound significance as a utopian ideal and a symbol of divine creation and eternal life.
subtitle: Land of Immortality and Paradise

keyMyths.title: Enki and Ninhursag
keyMyths.description: This myth describes the creation of Dilmun as a pure and blessed land by Enki. It details the events leading to the introduction of decay and disease into the once-perfect world through the actions of Ninhursag.

keyMyths.title: Epic of Gilgamesh
keyMyths.description: Gilgamesh seeks Utnapishtim, the survivor of the flood, who resides in Dilmun. He hopes to learn the secret of immortality, highlighting Dilmun's connection to eternal life and wisdom.

sources.author: Samuel Noah Kramer
sources.description: A foundational text on Sumerian mythology, providing detailed accounts of various myths and deities, including those associated with Dilmun.
sources.text: A foundational text on Sumerian mythology, providing detailed accounts of various myths and deities, including those associated with Dilmun.

sources.author: Thorkild Jacobsen
sources.work: The Treasures of Darkness: A History of Mesopotamian Religion
sources.description: Offers insights into the religious beliefs and practices of ancient Mesopotamia, shedding light on the cultural significance of Dilmun.
